## [00:00 - 03:02]

The fifth factor contributing to your success is frequency. So what do we talk about with frequency? This is an often debated metric that comes up. So you've got a certain number of people you've reached, you have a certain number of impressions, you divide those total impressions by the total people you've reached, you get a frequency, which means how many times per person on average, or how many times did the average person, or they reached? Is it once? Is it twice? Is it three times? Is it more? So frequency is how often you reach someone with an ad. Now people won't always act the first time they see an ad. So don't think that you just want to reach everybody once and you never want to reach them again. There is value in a certain amount of frequency. So some frequency is good. Eventually though, any additional views could be considered waste. Now it depends on your approach because obviously if you think about commercials as an example, we might see the same commercial 200 times it feels like. And we may be annoyed by that. But there's a reason why there's that much frequency. There's that brand recognition. There's that jingle that won't leave your head and somehow now you are thinking of this brand when you have a certain need. But that said, usually there's probably going to be more waste once you reach a certain point, especially with Facebook ads of frequency. If your frequency is too high, you're going to start getting more negative responses. Your CPM, which is a cost per thousand impressions will be going up and you're going to start wasting money. But if frequency is too low, people may not get a chance to act. So they may not really even see it the first time. They may have considered acting on your ad, but just think it around to it. They might need a reminder. So how should we approach this? Keep in mind, you have a lot of people say, oh, just have no more than two frequency of two or three or whatever it is. It's not, again, it's not a template saying there's a one good frequency because it depends on your goal, first of all. Higher frequency can be helpful if but your goal is awareness. But watch your budgets on smaller audiences because that's where you might end up with really high frequency and start increasing your waste. Ultimately, a monitor your CPM over time because that could be a sign. If your CPM is going up, it means your frequency is going up and you're just not reaching any new people, not getting any more results. It's just going to get more expensive just to keep showing your ad.

## [03:02 - 03:57]

And monitor the first time impression ratio, which is a ratio as you probably expect of the first time you've reached people. What percentage of people are you reaching today? Where's the first time you've reached them? Obviously, the higher that percentage, the fresher this ad is right now. Every time it reaches somebody. If that ratio is really high, that's a fresh ad. If it's very low, that means a lot of people are still seeing this ad for the second, third, fourth, or the time we don't necessarily know. So we do have this metric. You can find that within the inspect tool in general. Ultimately, this shouldn't be your gauge for success. It should be, is the ad still working? Because it's still working and you have really low first time impression. So be it. This is just a way of figuring out, okay, is frequency becoming an issue or not?
